projects.title: PI Integration Pipeline
projects.subtitle: Real-time Sensor Data Integration from AVEVA PI to Snowflake via Kafka
projects.description: Real-time streaming ELT pipeline ingesting sensor data from AVEVA PI System into Snowflake via PI Integrator for Business Analytics, Kafka Confluent, and Snowpipe — replacing slow on-prem PI server batch queries.
projects.long_description: Built a streaming data pipeline to move high-volume, real-time sensor data from the on-prem AVEVA PI System into Snowflake. Using PI Integrator for Business Analytics as the producer, Kafka Confluent as the message broker, and Snowpipe as the ingestion bridge, the pipeline delivers 100+ PI tags at 1-second intervals to Snowflake with sub-second latency.
projects.problem: The on-prem AVEVA PI System stored high-volume, real-time sensor data that became increasingly slow to load due to the sheer number of rows being continuously generated. The operations team needed a way to stream this data directly into Snowflake for analytics without relying on slow batch queries against the PI server.
projects.solution: Built a streaming pipeline using PI Integrator for Business Analytics as the producer, Kafka Confluent as the message broker, and Snowpipe as the ingestion bridge — delivering real-time sensor data into Snowflake with sub-second latency.

challenges.challenge: PI Integrator to Kafka configuration
challenges.solution: Set up proper topic destinations in PI Integrator, configured message schemas, and ensured Kafka producer settings matched downstream consumer expectations.

challenges.challenge: Kafka topic design and schema handling
challenges.solution: Used Confluent Schema Registry to manage and version message schemas, ensuring backward compatibility as the pipeline expanded to more sensor tags.

challenges.challenge: Snowpipe streaming configuration
challenges.solution: Configured the Kafka-Snowflake connector on the Confluent cluster, defined Snowflake tables with proper column mappings, and enabled Snowpipe for continuous streaming ingestion.

challenges.challenge: Snowflake schema design for streaming data
challenges.solution: Designed a raw/staging/final table structure with proper Snowflake clustering keys and auto-ingest settings to optimize query performance.

challenges.challenge: Scheduled data processing with Snowflake Tasks
challenges.solution: Created Snowflake Tasks with DAG-based dependencies to handle data transformation, deduplication, and aggregation on a configurable schedule.

results: Eliminated slow PI server batch queries by streaming data directly to Snowflake
Achieved sub-second real-time data latency from PI tag to Snowflake
Enabled real-time OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) dashboards for mine management
Integrated over 100 PI tags covering critical mining equipment
Zero data loss with continuous Snowpipe streaming ingestion

projects.title: ADF Watermark Pipeline
projects.subtitle: Automated Incremental Data Loading from SQL Server to Snowflake via Azure Data Factory
projects.description: Azure Data Factory pipeline that automates incremental data extraction from SQL Server into Snowflake using watermark-based change tracking — reducing manual pipeline management and enabling near-real-time data sync.
projects.long_description: Designed and built an ADF watermark pipeline that automatically extracts and loads new records from SQL Server into Snowflake based on reference watermark values. The pipeline leverages a For Each Loop to iterate over multiple tables, uses Lookup and If Condition activities to detect new data, and copies only changed records — significantly reducing pipeline complexity and processing overhead.
projects.problem: Multiple SQL Server tables required individual ADF pipelines for incremental loading, making pipeline management complex and difficult to scale as new source tables were added.
projects.solution: Designed a reusable watermark-based pipeline pattern that uses a For Each Loop to iterate over all watermark reference tables, automatically detecting and loading only new records into Snowflake — consolidating dozens of pipelines into a single managed solution.

challenges.challenge: Automating incremental load for multiple tables
challenges.solution: Built a For Each Loop-driven architecture that reads watermark reference tables and iterates through each table dynamically, applying Lookup and If Condition activities to detect new records.

challenges.challenge: Tracking watermark values across tables
challenges.solution: Maintained a dedicated WTMARK_DB schema containing watermark reference tables that store the last processed value for each source table, enabling stateless incremental loads.

challenges.challenge: Handling variable source table schemas
challenges.solution: Configured Copy Activity with dynamic source and sink mappings so new tables could be added to the watermark pipeline without modifying the ADF pipeline structure.

challenges.challenge: Ensuring reliable data copy without duplicates
challenges.solution: Implemented conditional copy logic: the Copy Activity only executes when new records are detected via the If Condition, preventing unnecessary writes and duplicate data.

results: Reduced dozens of individual ADF pipelines to a single reusable watermark pattern
Enabled automated incremental loads for multiple SQL Server tables into Snowflake
Eliminated manual intervention with watermark-based change tracking
Improved data freshness with near-real-time sync cadence
Easily scalable — new source tables added by updating watermark reference tables only

projects.title: Procurement Monitoring Dashboard
projects.subtitle: End-to-End Procurement Visibility from PR to Supplier Performance
projects.description: Built a comprehensive procurement monitoring system covering Purchase Requisition (PR), Control Tower, and Supplier Performance dashboards — enabling near real-time visibility into the entire procurement lifecycle from request to vendor delivery.
projects.long_description: Designed and delivered a full-stack procurement analytics solution that replaced manual, fragmented Excel reports with consolidated PowerBI dashboards. Led the complete data journey from business requirement gathering, Azure Data Factory ETL orchestration, Snowflake transformation and business logic, to executive-ready PowerBI visualizations for the procurement team.
projects.problem: The existing pipeline had a complex, rigid structure that made it difficult to customize or implement new changes. Redundant and unnecessary queries were impacting pipeline duration and performance, causing delays in data freshness for the procurement dashboards.
projects.solution: Redesigned the pipeline with a modular architecture and optimized SQL queries in Snowflake, improving pipeline performance and enabling faster iteration for new dashboard requirements. Built the end-to-end procurement monitoring solution covering PR workflows, budget tracking, and supplier scorecards served through interactive PowerBI dashboards.

challenges.challenge: Pipeline scalability and customization
challenges.solution: Redesigned the pipeline with a modular architecture, separating concerns into discrete, reusable components that allow easier customization and faster implementation of new requirements.

challenges.challenge: Pipeline performance optimization
challenges.solution: Identified and eliminated redundant and unnecessary queries through query optimization and refactoring, significantly reducing pipeline duration and improving data freshness.

challenges.challenge: Understanding complex procurement business logic across PR → PO → GRN lifecycle
challenges.solution: Collaborated closely with procurement team to map approval workflows, budget allocation hierarchies, and business rules into modular SQL transformations in Snowflake.

challenges.challenge: Budget tracking with variance alerts across cost centers
challenges.solution: Built budget vs actual tracking logic with real-time variance calculations, enabling procurement managers to identify over-budget situations before they escalate.

challenges.challenge: Handling multi-level PR approval workflow tracking
challenges.solution: Implemented approval stage tracking with timestamp logging, allowing the Control Tower dashboard to show PR progress through each approval gate in real-time.

results: Reduced pipeline processing time from ~50 minutes to ~20 minutes per cycle
Enabled faster iteration for new dashboard requirements with modular pipeline structure
Achieved near real-time procurement visibility reducing decision cycle time
Created standardized supplier performance scoring across Quality, Delivery, and Cost metrics
Supported PMO team in monitoring 500+ active PRs across multiple cost centers

projects.title: River Waste Calculator
projects.subtitle: Excel-Based Financial Estimation Tool for Wastewater Management
projects.description: Built an Excel-based financial estimation tool to help environmental professionals and waste management companies calculate, forecast, and optimize costs associated with water waste management operations.
projects.long_description: Developed a comprehensive Excel calculator with 150+ custom formulas covering OPEX, CAPEX, regulatory fees, and operational costs for wastewater management. The tool enables multi-scenario analysis, sensitivity analysis, and automated PDF report generation — reducing manual cost estimation from hours to minutes.
projects.problem: Environmental professionals and waste management companies relied on manual, error-prone spreadsheet calculations to estimate project costs for wastewater management — leading to inconsistent results, time-consuming iterations, and lack of standardized methodology across proposals.
projects.solution: Built a comprehensive Excel-based calculator with 150+ custom formulas that provides automated cost estimation, multi-scenario analysis, and professional PDF report generation — enabling users to generate accurate wastewater management cost estimates in minutes instead of hours.

challenges.challenge: Complex financial modeling with multiple cost components
challenges.solution: Designed a modular worksheet structure separating OPEX, CAPEX, regulatory fees, and operational costs — allowing users to understand and modify individual components without affecting the entire model.

challenges.challenge: Ensuring accuracy across 150+ interconnected formulas
challenges.solution: Implemented cross-sheet validation and error-checking mechanisms with built-in tooltips for each input field, ensuring data integrity while maintaining user-friendliness.

challenges.challenge: Multi-scenario analysis for different waste volumes and treatment methods
challenges.solution: Built scenario comparison functionality allowing users to test different waste volumes, treatment methods, and operational parameters side-by-side with automatic recalculation.

challenges.challenge: Regulatory compliance fee calculations
challenges.solution: Researched and integrated regulatory fee structures into the calculator, enabling users to estimate compliance costs and penalties based on local environmental regulations.

challenges.challenge: Professional report generation for client proposals
challenges.solution: Developed macro-assisted automation to generate professional PDF reports from calculated data, reducing manual report preparation time significantly.

results: Reduced manual cost estimation time from hours to minutes
Enabled multi-scenario analysis for comprehensive project planning
Created standardized methodology for wastewater cost estimation
Achieved 100% calculation accuracy through built-in validation
Provided professional PDF report generation for client proposals

projects.title: Routing App Weather Integration
projects.subtitle: Geospatial Data & Weather API Pipeline for Plantation Routing
projects.description: Built a weather-forecasting pipeline integrating geospatial data with 3rd-party weather APIs (Meteoblue, TWC) to support near real-time site and weather monitoring for the RGE Fiber Command Center routing application.
projects.long_description: Developed a Python-based pipeline on AWS that pulls geospatial data and weather forecasts from Meteoblue and The Weather Company (TWC) APIs, processing location-based weather data for 50+ plantation sites. The pipeline enables the Fiber Command Center to make data-driven decisions on field routing and replanting activities based on real-time weather conditions and 10-day forecasts.
projects.problem: Wood stock tracking from temporary field storage to mill sites lacked real-time visibility. Logistics teams had no unified system to track wood movements, monitor stock levels, and correlate transportation data with mill schedules — leading to delays, stock discrepancies, and inefficient fleet utilization.
projects.solution: Built a pipeline that integrates geospatial field data with weather forecasts and wood stock movements, enabling near real-time tracking from field temporary storage to mill sites. The solution provides logistics teams with unified visibility on stock levels, transportation status, and mill receiving capacity — optimizing wood flow and reducing stock discrepancies.

challenges.challenge: Tracking wood movements across multiple temporary field storage locations
challenges.solution: Implemented geospatial indexing for field storage locations, enabling real-time tracking of wood stock as it moves from temporary storage to mill sites.

challenges.challenge: Correlating transportation data with mill receiving schedules
challenges.solution: Built a unified data model that links transportation status with mill capacity and schedules, allowing logistics teams to anticipate bottlenecks and optimize fleet dispatch.

challenges.challenge: Weather-aware transportation planning for wood logistics
challenges.solution: Integrated weather forecasting to predict optimal transfer windows — determining which wood storage locations are near and low-risk for transport based on weather conditions.

challenges.challenge: Near real-time stock level visibility across locations
challenges.solution: Configured Databricks Delta Live Tables with incremental processing to maintain up-to-date stock levels while optimizing compute costs.

challenges.challenge: Handling multi-source data from field sensors and manual inputs
challenges.solution: Designed a robust data ingestion layer that normalizes data from various sources, handling inconsistencies and ensuring data quality across the pipeline.

results: Enabled real-time wood stock tracking from field storage to mill sites
Predicted optimal transfer windows based on weather conditions
Identified low-risk storage locations for efficient wood transport
Reduced stock discrepancies through unified visibility across locations
Provided logistics teams with weather-aware routing recommendations
